title: tetratheca hirsuta subsp. hirsuta
intro: Tetratheca hirsuta subsp. hirsuta, also known as the hairy pink-bells or hairy pygmy-pea, is a flowering plant species endemic to Western Australia. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Aboriginal culture, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Tetratheca hirsuta subsp. hirsuta has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional or experienced herbalist.
